feed.title: 赵京华网页 | Web page of Jing Hua Zhao
feed.subtitle: Web page

entry.title: R/4.5.3 is documented on CSD3
entry.content:
See < https://cambridge-ceu.github.io/csd3/applications/R.html>

Note that,

  • The series include R/4.5.3-icelake, R/4.5.3-gcc11, R/4.5.3-gcc12.
  • Since R_LIBS has R, R-gcc11/R-gcc12 those in their respective directories are recognised automattically.
  • In the case of R/4.5.3-gcc12, a fully functional gcc/12.5.0 is made, https://cambridge-ceu.github.io/csd3/systems/setup.html#fn:gcc.
    • There might be some residuals from using -lintl, therefore xgboost is experimented with LIBS=”-lintl”.
    • The issue goes away with gcc/12.5.0.
entry.summary: See < https://cambridge-ceu.github.io/csd3/applications/R.html> Note that, The series include R/4.5.3-icelake, R/4.5.3-gcc11, R/4.5.3-gcc12. Since R_LIBS has R, R-gcc11/R-gcc12 those in their respective directories are recognised automattically. In the case of R/4.5.3-gcc12, a fully functional gcc/12.5.0 is made, https://cambridge-ceu.github.io/csd3/systems/setup.html#fn:gcc. There might be some residuals from using -lintl, therefore xgboost is experimented with LIBS=”-lintl”. The issue goes away with gcc/12.5.0.

entry.title: gaawr2 0.0.7 is published on CRAN
entry.content:
See https://jinghuazhao.github.io/gaawr2/index.html.

Note that

  • The package vignettes are invisible from CRAN website but available from above.
  • Inside R, vignettes are visible from help.start().
entry.summary: See https://jinghuazhao.github.io/gaawr2/index.html. Note that The package vignettes are invisible from CRAN website but available from above. Inside R, vignettes are visible from help.start().

entry.title: gap 1.14 is on CRAN
entry.content:
The package vignette is moved to gap.examples.

06/02/2026

gap 1.9 is on CRAN, https://cran.r-project.org/package=gap.
entry.summary: The package vignette is moved to gap.examples. 06/02/2026 gap 1.9 is on CRAN, https://cran.r-project.org/package=gap.

entry.title: Henry-Stewart talks goes live
entry.content:
Updates

30/1/2026    Package gaawr2 0.0.5 is released and on CRAN.

URLs

  • HS Talks, https://hstalks.com/expert/1336/dr-jing-hua-zhao/?biosci.
  • CRAN, https://cloud.r-project.org/web/packages/gaawr2/index.html.
  • GitHub, https://github.com/jinghuazhao/gaawr2.
  • LinkedIn, https://www.linkedin.com/in/jinghua-zhao-2350b2209/
  • Web site, https://jinghuazhao.github.io/gaawr2/.

Citation (ChatGPT with minor revisions)

The Henry Stewart Talks series shows:

Zhao, J.H. (2026, January 28). Genetic association analysis with R: latest developments. Published in The Biomedical & Life Sciences Collection, Henry Stewart Talks. DOI: 10.69645/FRFQ9519. (HSTalks)

That DOI is the formal reference for this HSTalks lecture and is what you should cite if you want the latest development talk by Dr. Jing Hua Zhao (University of Cambridge) on genetic association analysis using R. (HSTalks)

APA style

Zhao, J. H. (2026, January 28). Genetic association analysis with R: latest developments [Video]. Henry Stewart Talks. https://doi.org/10.69645/FRFQ9519 (HSTalks)

MLA style

Zhao, Jing Hua. “Genetic Association Analysis with R: Latest Developments.” Henry Stewart Talks, 28 Jan. 2026, doi:10.69645/FRFQ9519. (HSTalks)

Vancouver style

Zhao JH. Genetic association analysis with R: latest developments [Internet]. Henry Stewart Talks; 2026 Jan 28 [cited 2026 Jan 28]. Available from: https://doi.org/10.69645/FRFQ9519 (HSTalks)

According to the summary, the talk covers:

  • Updated tools and workflows in R for genetic association analysis
  • R environment and software topics
  • GWAS packages, AI/machine-learning methods, biomaRt annotation
  • Package development and distribution
  • Shiny/GUI tools and web deployment of workflows (HSTalks)

Examples of recent R-related genetic association methods

  • AssocTests: A Journal of Statistical Software article on R tools for genetic association analysis methods. DOI: 10.18637/jss.v094.i05. (jstatsoft.org)

  • netgwas: Implements network-based genome-wide association methods in R. DOI: 10.32614/RJ-2023-011. (The R Journal)

  • topr: An R package for visualising and annotating genetic association results (BMC Bioinformatics 2023). (Springer)

If you want a compact list for inclusion in a manuscript:

Zhao JH. Genetic association analysis with R: latest developments. Henry Stewart Talks; 2026 Jan 28. doi:10.69645/FRFQ9519. (HSTalks) Behrouzi P, Arends D, Wit EC. netgwas: An R package for Network-Based Genome Wide Association Studies. The R Journal. 2023;14(4):18-37. doi:10.32614/RJ-2023-011. (The R Journal) AssocTests: An R Package for Genetic Association Studies. Journal of Statistical Software. 2020;94(5). doi:10.18637/jss.v094.i05. (jstatsoft.org) topr: an R package for viewing and annotating genetic association results. BMC Bioinformatics. 2023;24:268. (Springer)

If you want a short summary paragraph describing the current state of genetic association analysis in R with up-to-date references for a paper or talk, just let me know!
entry.summary: Updates 30/1/2026 Package gaawr2 0.0.5 is released and on CRAN. URLs HS Talks, https://hstalks.com/expert/1336/dr-jing-hua-zhao/?biosci. CRAN, https://cloud.r-project.org/web/packages/gaawr2/index.html. GitHub, https://github.com/jinghuazhao/gaawr2. LinkedIn, https://www.linkedin.com/in/jinghua-zhao-2350b2209/ Web site, https://jinghuazhao.github.io/gaawr2/. Citation (ChatGPT with minor revisions) The Henry Stewart Talks series shows: Zhao, J.H. (2026, January 28). Genetic association analysis with R: latest developments. Published in The Biomedical & Life Sciences Collection, Henry Stewart Talks. DOI: 10.69645/FRFQ9519. (HSTalks) That DOI is the formal reference for this HSTalks lecture and is what you should cite if you want the latest development talk by Dr. Jing Hua Zhao (University of Cambridge) on genetic association analysis using R. (HSTalks) APA style Zhao, J. H. (2026, January 28). Genetic association analysis with R: latest developments [Video]. Henry Stewart Talks. https://doi.org/10.69645/FRFQ9519 (HSTalks) MLA style Zhao, Jing Hua. “Genetic Association Analysis with R: Latest Developments.” Henry Stewart Talks, 28 Jan. 2026, doi:10.69645/FRFQ9519. (HSTalks) Vancouver style Zhao JH. Genetic association analysis with R: latest developments [Internet]. Henry Stewart Talks; 2026 Jan 28 [cited 2026 Jan 28]. Available from: https://doi.org/10.69645/FRFQ9519 (HSTalks) According to the summary, the talk covers: Updated tools and workflows in R for genetic association analysis R environment and software topics GWAS packages, AI/machine-learning methods, biomaRt annotation Package development and distribution Shiny/GUI tools and web deployment of workflows (HSTalks) Examples of recent R-related genetic association methods AssocTests: A Journal of Statistical Software article on R tools for genetic association analysis methods. DOI: 10.18637/jss.v094.i05. (jstatsoft.org) netgwas: Implements network-based genome-wide association methods in R. DOI: 10.32614/RJ-2023-011. (The R Journal) topr: An R package for visualising and annotating genetic association results (BMC Bioinformatics 2023). (Springer) If you want a compact list for inclusion in a manuscript: Zhao JH. Genetic association analysis with R: latest developments. Henry Stewart Talks; 2026 Jan 28. doi:10.69645/FRFQ9519. (HSTalks) Behrouzi P, Arends D, Wit EC. netgwas: An R package for Network-Based Genome Wide Association Studies. The R Journal. 2023;14(4):18-37. doi:10.32614/RJ-2023-011. (The R Journal) AssocTests: An R Package for Genetic Association Studies. Journal of Statistical Software. 2020;94(5). doi:10.18637/jss.v094.i05. (jstatsoft.org) topr: an R package for viewing and annotating genetic association results. BMC Bioinformatics. 2023;24:268. (Springer) If you want a short summary paragraph describing the current state of genetic association analysis in R with up-to-date references for a paper or talk, just let me know!
